evals.title: Kenya Class A Urban Rigid Pavement Design
evals.prompt: Design a rigid pavement for a new 4-lane Class A urban road in Nairobi with cumulative traffic of 50 MCESA, subgrade CBR of 5% (k-value 40 MPa/m), and available cement concrete with 4.5 MPa flexural strength. The road has fair drainage conditions. Specify the pavement type, slab thickness, joint design, reinforcement, and subbase requirements.

assertions.description: Must reference Kenya RDM 3.5 as design standard
Must use Kenya slab type terminology (JUC, JRC, or CRCP)
Must use Kenya CESA traffic loading terminology
Must provide a slab thickness value in mm
Must address joint layout or reinforcement requirements
Must specify Kenya subbase requirements
Must flag output as preliminary/screening level
Slab thickness should be 225-300 mm for 50 MCESA at k=40 MPa/m

evals.title: Universal Toll Highway -- JPCP vs CRCP Comparison
evals.prompt: Design a rigid pavement for a toll highway in a generic tropical country with 80 million ESALs, subgrade k=50 MPa/m, concrete flexural strength 4.0 MPa. Compare JPCP and CRCP options. For each option, provide slab thickness, joint design (or reinforcement for CRCP), and subbase. Recommend the preferred option with justification.

assertions.description: Must use AASHTO 1993 or PCA universal design method
Must cover JPCP option
Must cover CRCP option
Must provide thickness for both JPCP and CRCP
Must discuss joint design for JPCP and absence of joints for CRCP
Must discuss CRCP reinforcement requirements
Must provide a recommendation with justification
JPCP thickness should be 250-325 mm for 80M ESALs at k=50

evals.title: Low-Volume Urban Road -- Block Paving / Cobblestone Edge Case
evals.prompt: A low-volume urban road (0.5 MCESA, 40 km/h design speed) in a developing country needs a durable, low-maintenance surface. Local materials include natural stone and concrete blocks but limited asphalt supply. Recommend a rigid pavement type and provide the design. Consider labour availability, maintenance requirements, and long-term durability.

assertions.description: Must consider block paving and/or cobblestone as options

assertions.value: low speed OR low traffic OR low volume
assertions.description: Must account for low speed and low traffic context
Must discuss labour-intensive construction advantage
Must flag maintenance considerations

assertions.value: durability OR service life OR long-term
assertions.description: Must address long-term durability
Must specify structural layers below the surface
Should NOT recommend CRCP for 0.5 MCESA -- grossly overdesigned

assertions.value: preliminary OR screening OR professional review
assertions.description: Must flag output as preliminary level
